title: Sutera revoluta
taxonomy: Sutera revoluta is classified in the family Scrophulariaceae. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes mint, figwort, and snapdragon. The order Lamiales is further classified under the clade Asterids, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes daisies and sunflowers. The clade Asterids is part of the larger clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. Sutera revoluta is a flowering plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world.

intro: Sutera revoluta, also known as the 'Scorpion's Tail', is a flowering plant species within the Lamiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Sutera revoluta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sutera revoluta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat fever and respiratory infections.
